stories.title: Yemen's Houthi rebels enter the fighting in the Middle East
stories.description: At least 15 U.S. military personnel were wounded in an Iranian attack on Saudi Arabia and Yemen's Houthi rebels entered the war with an attack on Israel.

stories.title: Over a dozen U.S. soldiers injured in attack on Saudi base as Iran-backed Houthis enter war
stories.description: An Iranian strike on an air base in Saudi Arabia wounded at least 15 U.S. service members. Israel also said it intercepted a missile launched from Iran-backed Houthi rebels in Yemen.

stories.title: Senate votes to fund most of DHS. And, Trump extends Iran's deadline to reopen strait
stories.description: The Senate has voted to fund most of the Department of Homeland Security. And, President Trump extends the deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Iranian missile hits an airbase in Saudi Arabia, injuring U.S. troops
stories.description: A U.S. official said several U.S. service members were wounded in the strike and some aircraft were apparently damaged as well. Iran has targeted U.S. bases throughout the region since the war began.

stories.title: Rubio meets with other G7 nations for first time since Iran war
stories.description: Secretary of State Rubio meets his G7 colleagues in Paris for the first time since the start of the war on Iran.

stories.title: Iran-backed Houthis join war with attack against Israel
stories.description: There are fears the Iranian proxy may attack shipping in the Red Sea, adding to global economic woes.

stories.title: Potential Houthi threat to Red Sea shipping could further damage global economy
stories.description: The Iran-backed group could bring a second crucial waterway to a standstill, writes Sebastian Usher.

stories.title: Three Lebanese journalists killed in Israeli strike, say broadcasters
stories.description: The Israeli military confirms it killed Ali Shoeib from the Hezbollah-affiliated Al Manar TV.

stories.title: 'My daughter is under the rubble': Inside Tehran as civilian toll of strikes rises
stories.description: One month since the US and Israel launched strikes on Iran, Tehran residents tell the BBC their lives have been devastated.

stories.title: Gaza peace doubts deepen as attention shifts to Iran
stories.description: With the world focused on the Iran war, there is increasing uncertainty about what happens next for Gaza.

stories.title: Palestinian man killed as death toll from West Bank settler violence climbs
stories.description: Mohammad al-Malhi is the seventh Palestinian killed by a settler in a surge of violence since the start of the war with Iran.

stories.title: UN human rights chief calls on US to conclude probe into Iran school strike
stories.description: The strike - which killed at least 168 people, mostly children - "evoked a visceral horror", Volker Türk said.

stories.title: Ukraine signs deal with Saudi Arabia offering drone expertise
stories.description: Volodymyr Zelensky says the Gulf kingdom faces the same type of attacks from Iran that Kyiv has been resisting.

stories.title: Israel says it has killed Iran's navy chief overseeing Strait of Hormuz blockade
stories.description: Israel's defence minister said a number of other "senior Navy command officials" have also been killed. Iran has not yet commented.

stories.title: Trump's Iran strategy is to pursue two off-ramps at once
stories.description: The White House insists the president is dictating events, but even some Republicans are frustrated by mixed messaging about his war aims.

stories.title: Video shows US missile likely used in deadly strike on Iranian town, experts say
stories.description: The analysts identified a Precision Strike Missile (PrSM) in footage of the strike on the southern town of Lamerd.

stories.title: Is Trump's pause on attacking Iranian energy for diplomacy or an escalation?
stories.description: The US president's commitment to deadlines is fluid but he uses them for a purpose, writes the BBC's James Landale.

stories.title: How the Hormuz closure could affect food, medicines and smartphones
stories.description: The price of a wide range of goods - from food, to smartphones, to medicines - could be affected by the US-Israel war with Iran.

stories.title: Why did US and Israel attack Iran and how long could the war last?
stories.description: Conflict has spread across the Middle East since the US and Israel launched strikes on Iran on 28 February.

stories.title: A committed pharmacist and a homesick blogger – the Iranian civilians killed in the war
stories.description: The toll on civilians is mounting fast as the US-Israeli war on Iran continues.

stories.title: Yemen’s Houthis launch ballistic missile at Israel as Middle East conflict escalates
stories.description: Yemen’s Houthi rebels fired a ballistic missile towards Israel on Saturday, marking their entry into the intensifying Middle East war. The move comes amid rising regional tensions, US-Israeli airstrikes on Iran, and disruptions to Red Sea shipping that threaten global trade and e...

stories.title: Iranian strike on Saudi base wounds several US troops, damages aircraft
stories.description: A dozen US troops were wounded in an Iranian attack on Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia, with two suffering serious injuries and 10 concussions, according to US media reports. The personnel were inside a building hit during the strike, which involved at least one missile an...

stories.title: What could Houthi involvement in the Iran war mean?
stories.description: Iranian-backed Houthi rebels entered the month-old war in the Middle East on Saturday by claiming a missile launch that Israel said it intercepted. The Houthis’ entry could further hurt global shipping if they again target vessels in the Bab el-Mandeb Strait off the Red Sea. FRAN...

stories.title: 'Divide among men above and under 35': CPAC highlights generational GOP split
stories.description: For the first time in nearly a decade, President Donald Trump did not attend one of the biggest annual meetings of conservatives. But even in his absence, the Conservative Political Action Conference revolved around him as well as the Iran war which has divided Trump supporters. ...

stories.title: Israeli strike hits Tehran home, killing several children
stories.description: Israeli authorities said an Iranian missile attack killed a man in Tel Aviv on Friday, as Tehran pressed its retaliatory strikes across the region a month into the war. Meanwhile in southern Tehran, a pre-dawn Israeli strike hit a residential building, killing at least five child...

stories.title: Air strikes in Iraq kill three PMF fighters, two police
stories.description: Iraq turning into 'expanding battleground' in war, which began on February 28 with US-Israeli strikes on Iran.

stories.title: Reza Pahlavi pledges to ‘make Iran great again’ at 2026 CPAC conference
stories.description: Iranian opposition leader calls on Trump administration to 'stay the course' as the US and Israel wage war on Iran.

stories.title: Pakistan secures Iran deal to send 20 ships through Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Islamabad's diplomatic push bears fruit as the world watches for signs of a broader breakthrough.

stories.title: Kuwait airport hit by Iranian drone strikes
stories.description: Thick, black smoke rose from Kuwait International Airport Saturday after suspected Iranian drone strikes caused damage.

stories.title: Could Ukraine’s drone expertise serve Gulf countries?
stories.description: Gulf nations seek new defence partnerships as Iran war escalates.

stories.title: As war rages, Iranian politicians push for exit from nuclear weapons treaty
stories.description: While US-Israeli attacks hit key infrastructure, hardliners demand withdrawal from Nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty.

stories.title: One month in, disapproval high but US lawmakers take no action on Iran war
stories.description: Republicans and Democrats remain entrenched despite public polling showing dismay over war, petrol prices.

stories.title: Iran warns neighbours not to let ‘enemies run the war’ from their land
stories.description: President Pezeshkian's warning is the latest by Tehran to neighbours not to allow their territory to be used in the war.

stories.title: At least 15 US troops wounded in Iran strike on Saudi airbase: Reports
stories.description: At least five soldiers are reported in serious condition following Iran attack that damaged refuelling aircraft.

stories.title: Iran footballers pay tribute to victims of school strike at start of war
stories.description: The Iran national team played for the first time since the Middle East conflict began, losing 2-1 to Nigeria.

stories.title: Former US defence chief says more war won’t bring peace to Middle East
stories.description: Leon Panetta, former US Secretary of Defence, says that there is no clear end to the war in Iran.

stories.title: Ukraine’s Zelensky agrees to air defence cooperation with UAE, Qatar on Gulf tour
stories.description: Qatar and Ukraine signed a defence agreement on Saturday, which includes cooperation on countering threats from missiles and drones, the Gulf state’s government said, as Iran presses an aerial campaign against its neighbours. Earlier on Saturday, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zel...

stories.title: ‘Blatant crime’: Israeli strike kills 3 journalists in southern Lebanon
stories.description: An Israeli strike killed three journalists on Saturday in south Lebanon, with authorities denouncing the attack as a “war crime”. Lebanese television news channel Al Manar said its reporter Ali Shoaib and reporter Fatima Ftouni, from Lebanese pan-Arab broadcaster Al Mayadeen, wer...

stories.title: Foreign ministers of Saudi Arabia, Turkiye, Egypt to visit Islamabad for ‘in-depth’ talks on Iran war: FO
stories.description: ISLAMABAD: Pakistan is set to host the foreign ministers of Saudi Arabia, Turkiye and Egypt from March 29-30 for “in-depth discussions” on a variety of issues, including efforts to reduce the ongoing tensions in the region amid the US-Israeli war on Iran. In a press release issue...

stories.title: Trump says ‘Cuba is next’, denies losing MAGA support
stories.description: United States President Donald Trump said on Friday that “Cuba is next” in his latest threat against the Caribbean island, as he dismissed concerns that recent American military actions would cost him political support. Cuba’s communist government has faced mounting pressure from...

stories.title: Attacks on Iran’s N-plants, steelworks belie ‘messaging’
stories.description: • IAEA voices concern as US-Israeli air strikes hit uranium plant, heavy water facility• Araghchi vows to exact heavy price; IRGC says industrial sites in region are now targets• UN forms task force to keep ships moving thru Hormuz as Guards vow to block ‘enemy vessels’• Rubio co...

stories.title: US Congress panel demands full public hearing on Iran war as concerns mount
stories.description: WASHINGTON: Democrats on the United States House Armed Services Committee have called for an immediate public hearing on President Donald Trump’s war against Iran, pressing the Pentagon to testify publicly as concerns mount over strategy, transparency, and the mounting human and ...

stories.title: UNSC holds closed-door session on Iran school bombing
stories.description: WASHINGTON: The United Nations Security Council held a closed-door meeting on Friday at Russia’s request to discuss US-Israeli strikes on civilian sites in Iran, diplomatic sources told Dawn. Russia demanded the meeting regarding US-Israeli actions in Iran. Earlier, Iran had also...

stories.title: Israel has uprooted 370,000 children in Lebanon: UN
stories.description: • Global body warns country at risk of ‘humanitarian catastrophe’• Tel Aviv carries out fresh strikes on Beirut’s southern suburbs• Hezbollah says its fighters clashed with Israeli forces at two villages in the south GENEVA / BEIRUT: More than 370,000 children have been forced fr...

stories.title: G7 allies press Marco Rubio on US plans for Iran war
stories.description: • UK FM urges swift resolution to conflict that restores regional stability• At World Trade Organisation meeting, US faces pressure to clarify trade policies CERNAY VILLE: G7 allies were on Friday pressing US Secretary of State Marco Rubio for clarity on American plans for Iran a...

stories.title: Iran-linked hackers breach FBI Director Kash Patel’s personal email, publish excerpts online
stories.description: Iran-linked hackers on Friday claimed they had accessed FBI Director Kash Patel’s personal email inbox, publishing photographs of the director and other documents to the internet. On their website, the hacker group Handala Hack Team said Patel “will now find his name among the li...

stories.title: China blasts ‘false’ news after report says chipmaker supplying Iran
stories.description: China’s foreign ministry accused the media of publishing “false information” on Friday following a report that said the country’s top semiconductor firm has sent chipmaking tools to Iran. The report, which cited information from two unidentified senior officials in US President D...

stories.title: US can only confirm about a third of Iran’s missile arsenal destroyed, sources say
stories.description: The United States can only determine with certainty that it has destroyed about a third of Iran’s vast missile arsenal as the US and Israeli war on the country ​nears its one-month mark, according to five people familiar with the US intelligence. The status of around another thir...

stories.title: Rubio says war on Iran to last ‘weeks not months’, no US ground troops needed
stories.description: The war in the Gulf is still expected to last weeks, not months, and Washington can meet all its objectives without using ground troops, US Secretary of State Marco Rubio said on Friday. Rubio told reporters after meeting G7 counterparts in France that Washington was “on or ahead...

stories.title: China says US-Iran talks would help restore Hormuz transit, supports Pakistan as mediator
stories.description: Initiating peace talks in the war on Iran “is no easy task,” but it would be “conducive to restoring normal navigation through the Strait of Hormuz,” Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi said during a call with Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar on Friday. The two “...

stories.title: Sony to hike PlayStation 5 prices again as memory chip costs surge
stories.description: Sony Group is raising global prices of its PlayStation 5 consoles, including a $100 increase in the US, marking its second hike in less than a year as the Japanese firm grapples with rising costs of key components such as memory chips. The tech industry’s race to build out artifi...

stories.title: Iran-Israel war LIVE: Iran sceptical over diplomatic efforts to stop war as Houthi involvement threatens global shipping
stories.description: Iran President lauds Pakistan for mediation to stop U.S., Israel 'aggression'; significant damage to Kuwait airport radar in drone attack: authorities

stories.title: Pedro Sanchez | Europe’s only anti-war Prime Minister
stories.description: The Spanish leader, who describes Israel’s atrocities in Gaza as genocide, has characterised the U.S.-Israel war on Iran as a violation of international law, becoming the only European head of government to publicly reject the war on principle

stories.title: Mohammad Bagher Zolqadr | Ascent of the Guardsman
stories.description: The rise of the former IRGC commander as the Secretary of Iran’s National Security Council suggests the Guards are tightening their grip on Iran’s state machinery

stories.title: Iranian attack on Saudi base causes American casualties; more U.S. forces arrive in West Asia
stories.description: Iran has kept up retaliatory attacks on Gulf nations it accuses of serving as a launchpad for U.S. strikes on the country, which began in a joint operation with Israel on February 28.

stories.title: Joe Kent | The rebel within
stories.description: The former soldier who has resigned as the National Counterterrorism chief over the war on Iran says Israel and its powerful lobby led the U.S. into the conflict

stories.title: Israeli airstrike in southern Lebanon kills 3 journalists covering the war
stories.description: The Israeli military said that it had targeted journalist Shoeib, accusing him of being a Hezbollah intelligence operative without providing evidence

stories.title: Iran’s uranium enrichment secures independence, peaceful atom — supreme leader
stories.description: Ali Khamenei added that the US and other countries are not interested in Iran's progress and have put forward conditions presupposing a complete halt of uranium enrichment on Iranian territory

stories.title: Tehran could weigh nuclear deal with US with Iran-based enrichment consortium — Axios
stories.description: According to the report, a sixth round of talks on the Iranian nuclear program between the US president’s Special Envoy Steve Witkoff and Iran’s top diplomat, Abbas Araghchi, could be held this weekend in a Middle Eastern country

stories.title: DPRK slams U.S. for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang
stories.description: The Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) has slammed the United States for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang.
